name: Wa'ahila Ridge Trail
tagline: A proper ridge hike that starts right behind the University of Hawaii — lunch break adventure material.
parking: Free parking at Wa'ahila Ridge State Recreation Area off Ruth Place — small lot, comes with restrooms and picnic tables
bestTime: Late afternoon for golden hour light on the ridge — but start early enough to finish before gate closes
talkStory: About 5 miles round trip with 1,000 ft of elevation gain along a narrow ridge with views into both Manoa and Palolo valleys. The first mile through the ironwood and Cook pine forest is gentle, then it gets real — narrow ridgeline with drop-offs on both sides. The views of Diamond Head, Waikiki, and the Ko'olau Range are incredible. Trail is well-maintained by the state but gets slippery when wet. One of the best easily-accessible ridge hikes from town — students at UH hit this between classes.
